zotero.abstractNote: Moving object segmentation is a fundamental task in biological and computer vision. While classical models rely on spatio-temporal energy filtering, recent deep learning approaches often use feature matching. Here, we compare these two approaches on a challenging dataset of naturalistic videos. We find that deep feature matching outperforms spatio-temporal energy filtering, especially in the presence of complex backgrounds and varying illumination. Our results suggest that deep feature matching is a promising direction for robust moving object segmentation.
